quizzes.title: Solitaire Cipher
quizzes.summary: Implement the deck-based Solitaire hand cipher. Normalize text, generate a keystream by repeatedly transforming a 54-card deck, and use the stream to encrypt or decrypt letters.
requirements: Represent the two jokers distinctly.
Apply joker moves, the triple cut, and the count cut in the documented order.
Skip joker-valued output cards.
Support both encryption and decryption with deterministic test decks.
approaches: Model every deck transformation as a pure function returning a new deck.
Use a small state object containing the deck and emitted keystream.
For performance experiments, compare immutable rebuilding with an actor-owned mutable deck capability.
quizzes.sample:
plaintext:  CODE IN RUBY LIVELONG
normalized: CODEINRUBYLIVELONG
operation: encrypt, then decrypt back to the normalized text

quizzes.title: Secret Santas
quizzes.summary: Assign every participant exactly one recipient while preventing self-assignment and same-family assignment. Produce a complete one-to-one matching or report that the constraints are impossible.
requirements: Each giver appears once.
Each recipient appears once.
No giver receives their own name.
No assignment stays within the same family.
Return an explicit error when no valid matching exists.
approaches: Shuffle-and-retry is simple but nondeterministic and can loop.
Backtracking with early constraint checks is deterministic and testable.
Bipartite matching scales better when the rule set grows.
quizzes.sample:
Ada Lovelace -> Grace Hopper
Grace Hopper -> Alan Turing
Alan Turing -> Ada Lovelace

quizzes.title: GEDCOM Parser
quizzes.summary: Parse GEDCOM lines whose leading level numbers describe hierarchy. Build a tree that preserves tags, optional identifiers, values, and parent-child relationships, then serialize the result as structured data.
requirements: Reject malformed level numbers.
A line may increase by only one level at a time.
Preserve repeated tags and ordering.
Do not confuse pointer identifiers with ordinary values.
approaches: Maintain a stack of the most recent node at each level.
First produce flat records, then perform a separate tree-building pass.
Stream large files and emit completed subtrees when their level closes.
quizzes.sample:
0 @I1@ INDI
1 NAME Ada /Lovelace/
1 BIRT
2 DATE 10 DEC 1815

quizzes.title: Countdown Numbers
quizzes.summary: Given six numbers and a target, combine some or all inputs with addition, subtraction, multiplication, and exact division. Each input may be used at most once. Find an exact expression or the closest reachable value.
requirements: Use each selected input no more than once.
Allow only positive intermediate values.
Allow division only when it is exact.
Prefer exact results; otherwise report the smallest absolute error.
approaches: Recursively choose unordered pairs and replace them with each legal result.
Memoize canonical multisets to avoid exploring equivalent states.
Use branch-and-bound once a near-target result is known.
quizzes.sample:
numbers: 75, 2, 8, 5, 10, 10
target: 926
result: an expression and its evaluated value

quizzes.title: Scrabble Stems
quizzes.summary: Find six-letter stems that can accept many different seventh letters and rearrange into valid seven-letter dictionary words. Rank stems by the number of distinct extension letters.
requirements: Treat anagrams as the same signature.
Count distinct extension letters, not total words.
Accept a minimum cutoff.
Return deterministic ranking and tie-breaking.
approaches: For every seven-letter word, remove each position and sort the remaining six letters.
Index words by sorted signature before deriving stems.
Use byte-frequency signatures to avoid repeated string sorting.
quizzes.sample: dictionary words -> stem signature -> distinct extension letters -> matching words

quizzes.title: Cryptograms
quizzes.summary: Solve a monoalphabetic substitution cryptogram with a dictionary. Words with the same repeated-letter pattern are candidates; assignments must maintain a one-to-one mapping between cipher and plain letters.
requirements: Preserve spaces and punctuation.
A cipher letter maps to exactly one plain letter.
Two cipher letters cannot map to the same plain letter.
Return all solutions or an explicit ambiguity/no-solution result.
approaches: Group dictionary words by canonical repetition pattern.
Choose the unsolved word with the fewest candidates first.
Propagate letter assignments and backtrack immediately on conflicts.
quizzes.sample:
cipher:  IFMMP XPSME
expected shape: HELLO WORLD (for an illustrative shifted example)

quizzes.title: LCD Numbers
quizzes.summary: Render decimal digits as scalable seven-segment LCD characters. The requested size controls horizontal segment width and vertical segment height.
requirements: Support digits 0 through 9.
Use a default size when none is supplied.
Reject non-positive sizes.
Keep exactly one column of space between rendered digits.
approaches: Represent each digit as a seven-bit segment mask.
Use three logical rows, expanding vertical rows by size.
Precompute glyph rows for repeated rendering.
quizzes.sample:
input: size=2, digits=012
output: a multi-line seven-segment display

quizzes.title: Roman Numerals
quizzes.summary: Convert integers from 1 through 3999 to canonical Roman numerals and convert valid canonical Roman numerals back to integers.
requirements: Emit subtractive forms such as IV, IX, XL, XC, CD, and CM.
Reject zero, negative, and values above 3999.
Reject non-canonical or malformed Roman input.
Round-trip every accepted value.
approaches: Greedily consume descending numeral/value pairs for encoding.
Parse pairs before single symbols for decoding.
Validate decoding by re-encoding and comparing with the input.

quizzes.title: Knight's Travails
quizzes.summary: Find a shortest legal path for a chess knight between two squares while avoiding forbidden squares. Return the path, or no result when the destination cannot be reached.
requirements: Use ordinary knight moves.
Stay on the 8x8 board.
Never land on a forbidden square.
Return a shortest path, including start and destination.
approaches: Breadth-first search guarantees a shortest unweighted path.
Store predecessor links instead of copying every partial path.
A bidirectional search reduces work for larger generalized boards.
quizzes.sample:
start: a8
finish: b7
forbidden: b6
result: one shortest legal path

quizzes.title: Mad Libs
quizzes.summary: Build a tiny template engine for Mad Libs. Locate labeled placeholders, request one value per unique label, and render the completed story while reusing repeated answers.
requirements: Recognize placeholders without evaluating code.
Prompt once for each unique label.
Reuse the same answer when a label repeats.
Preserve all non-placeholder text exactly.
approaches: Scan delimiters directly with string indices.
Tokenize the template into literal and placeholder records.
Separate parsing from answer collection so browser and CLI interfaces share the renderer.
quizzes.sample:
template: The (adjective) actor greeted the (noun).
answers: adjective=curious, noun=compiler

quizzes.title: Amazing Mazes
quizzes.summary: Generate a rectangular perfect maze: every cell is reachable and exactly one simple path connects any pair of cells. Render it in a deterministic text form for a supplied seed.
requirements: Honor runtime width and height.
Visit every cell.
Create no cycles.
Produce the same maze for the same seed.
approaches: Randomized depth-first search creates a spanning tree.
Randomized Kruskal builds passages by joining disjoint sets.
Recursive division starts with open space and adds walls.
quizzes.sample: width=8, height=5, seed=42 -> deterministic ASCII maze

quizzes.title: Index and Query
quizzes.summary: Build a small full-text index over named documents, then answer term queries without rescanning every document. Support conjunction, disjunction, and deterministic result ranking.
requirements: Normalize case and punctuation consistently.
Store each term only once per document in the posting list.
Support AND and OR queries.
Return document identifiers in a deterministic order.
approaches: Use an inverted index from token to document identifiers.
Store term frequencies to rank results.
Persist a compact index and update only changed documents.
quizzes.sample:
documents: three short texts
query: actors AND messages
result: matching document names
